campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review transportation funding investments from March 2026 and discussed a new policy restricting use of city property for federal civil enforcement activities. The council also addressed current job openings and strategies to recruit and keep city employees.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority investment report for March 2026
New policy prohibiting city property use for federal civil enforcement
City job vacancies and employee recruitment/retention efforts

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a meeting to address dangerous or neglected properties in Saratoga that need cleanup or repair. They heard from residents who had concerns about the county's list of problem properties and voted to move forward with fixing them. The council also adopted new policies on how the city handles technology changes and how residents can request special accommodations from the city.
keyTopics: Public hearing on cleanup orders for hazardous/neglected properties identified by the county
Approval to proceed with abatement work on problem properties
New Technology Disruption Policy adopted to replace outdated rules
Process established for residents to request reasonable accommodations from the city

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on May 5, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and address several infrastructure and safety matters. The council declared overgrown brush and hazardous vegetation as public nuisances and approved a landscape maintenance report. They also authorized the town manager to sign multi-year service agreements to keep city operations running smoothly.
keyTopics: Declared hazardous brush and overgrown vegetation a public nuisance
Approved landscape and lighting maintenance engineer's report
Authorized five-year service agreements with contractors
Approved April 21, 2026 meeting minutes

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize their upcoming agendas and schedule. They reviewed what items will be discussed at their May 19th meeting, set time limits for certain topics if needed, and approved their meeting calendar for the next fiscal year.
keyTopics: Finalized the May 19, 2026 City Council meeting agenda
Approved the fiscal year 2026-2027 meeting schedule
Made new City Council appointments to committees, boards, and commissions
Processed a travel request from Councilmember Foley

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: This May 14, 2026 Mountain View City Council meeting notice explains how residents can participate by submitting questions ahead of time. The council is meeting to handle various board and committee agendas, with locations posted at least 24 hours beforehand.
keyTopics: Submit questions online before the meeting using the provided link
Meeting locations posted on agendas 24+ hours in advance
Contact City Manager's Office with questions (650-903-6301 or CNC@MountainView.gov)
View agenda and staff reports at City Clerk's Office, 500 Castro Street

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-door meeting at 5 P.M. followed by a regular council meeting at 7 P.M. on May 5th, 2026. The closed session discussed confidential matters that weren't publicly disclosed. The regular meeting included a flag salute and appeared to include a small business-related agenda item.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting (specific topics not publicly disclosed)
Small business agenda item
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held an initial budget planning session for the 2026-27 fiscal year to discuss the city's proposed spending and finances. The meeting also covered lobbyist registration rules that require people trying to influence city decisions to officially register and report their activities.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal and financial planning
Lobbyist registration and disclosure requirements
Rules for people attempting to influence city legislative or administrative decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on May 14, 2026 covered important legal notices for residents. The council reminded the public that if you want to challenge any City decisions in court, you have 90 days from the date the decision is finalized to file a lawsuit.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline for filing legal challenges to City decisions
Quasi-adjudicative decisions subject to State law (California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6)
Legal notice requirements for residents

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve long-term spending plans for the city, including a five-year capital improvement budget and next year's operating budget. The council also adopted a master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and made updates to how council meetings are run.
keyTopics: 2027-2031 Capital Improvement Plan - major city projects and infrastructure spending approved
FY 2026-27 Operating Budget - how the city will spend money on day-to-day services
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan - new parks and walking/biking paths for the area
City Council Procedures updates - changes to how meetings are conducted

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The city council held a public hearing on a proposal to build a 6-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city parking lot. All units would be affordable housing, and the developer is asking for special zoning allowances under state affordable housing law.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units on a small downtown lot (0.43 acres)
Replacing city-owned surface parking lot (Lot T with 52 spaces)
Six-story building with approximately 76,400 square feet
Request for density bonus concessions under California state law
